_meta.disclaimer: Scores are based on automated analysis of publicly available Terms of Service documents under EU jurisdiction. Results are provided for informational purposes only and do not constitute legal advice. Analysis is based on document versions available at generation date; terms may have changed since.

schema.lookup: Each service maps directly to its EU profile. _ref entries are aliases (e.g. instagram.com → facebook.com).

flags.title: Data sharing with third parties
flags.quote: we may share your information with our affiliates and business partners

flags.title: AI model training on user data
flags.quote: we use the information we collect to train and improve our AI models

flags.title: Pre-upload content collection
flags.quote: we collect information about content you create, even before you post it

flags.title: Biometric data collection
flags.quote: we may collect biometric identifiers including face and voice data

flags.title: Location & behavioral tracking
flags.quote: we collect your approximate or precise location information

flags.title: Silent terms changes
flags.quote: we will notify users of material changes in advance

credits.title: GDPR compliance
credits.note: GDPR applies; TikTok is under DSA regulation and has faced EU enforcement actions

credits.title: Data deletion right
credits.note: GDPR Article 17 right to erasure applies

tiktok.com.jurisdictionNote: TikTok's EU terms are subject to GDPR and the EU Digital Services Act (DSA). Binding arbitration is not applicable under EU consumer law. However, data practices remain deeply concerning.

flags.title: Data sharing with third parties
flags.quote: we share information with third-party partners and advertisers

flags.title: AI model training on user data
flags.quote: your data may be used to train our AI systems

flags.quote: we receive information about you from third-party websites and apps

flags.title: Deleted data not truly erased
flags.quote: deletion may take up to 90 days from backup systems

flags.title: Location & behavioral tracking

credits.title: GDPR compliance
credits.note: Meta has been fined multiple times by EU DPAs — compliance is enforced

credits.title: Data portability
credits.note: Download Your Information available per GDPR Art. 20

credits.title: Data deletion right
credits.note: GDPR Art. 17 right to erasure

facebook.com.jurisdictionNote: Meta has faced repeated GDPR enforcement actions in the EU (Ireland DPC, DPAs in Germany, France). EU users have stronger rights but the company's data practices remain a concern.

flags.title: Broad content license
flags.quote: you grant X a broad, royalty-free license to make your Content available to the rest of the world

flags.title: AI model training on user data
flags.quote: your content may be used to train our machine learning models

flags.title: Termination without cause
flags.quote: X may suspend or terminate your account at any time

flags.title: Liability capped at €100
flags.quote: X's total liability shall not exceed €100 or the amount you paid

flags.title: Silent terms changes
flags.quote: your continued access to the Services after changes constitutes acceptance

credits.title: GDPR compliance
credits.note: X Corp operates xAI under EU GDPR; subject to DSA regulation

credits.title: Data deletion right
credits.note: GDPR Art. 17 right to erasure applies

x.com.jurisdictionNote: Binding arbitration and class action waivers are unenforceable under EU consumer law. However, the broad content license, AI training, and €100 liability cap remain deeply concerning.

credits.title: No binding arbitration

google.com.jurisdictionNote: Google's EU terms do not include mandatory arbitration. EU users have stronger data rights under GDPR, including opt-out for AI training.

credits.title: GDPR compliance
credits.note: Netflix Netherlands B.V. processes EU user data under GDPR

credits.title: 30-day advance notice
credits.note: EU terms require 30 days notice before material changes

credits.title: No binding arbitration
credits.note: EU terms do not include binding arbitration clauses

credits.title: Data deletion right
credits.note: GDPR Art. 17 applies

netflix.com.jurisdictionNote: Netflix's EU terms are significantly more favorable than US terms. No mandatory arbitration, 30-day advance notice of changes, and GDPR rights apply.

flags.title: Fund holds
flags.quote: PayPal may place holds on funds pending investigation

flags.title: Data sharing with third parties
flags.quote: we share personal data with service providers acting on our behalf

flags.title: Termination without cause
flags.quote: we may limit or close your account with 2 months notice

credits.title: GDPR compliance
credits.note: PayPal (Europe) S.à r.l. is regulated by the Luxembourg CSSF and operates under GDPR

credits.title: 2-month advance notice
credits.note: EU Payment Services Directive requires 2 months advance notice of changes

credits.title: Fund holds capped
credits.note: EU Payment Services Directive limits hold duration and requires justification

credits.title: Formal appeal process
credits.note: EU regulated — complaints to Luxembourg CSSF and national financial ombudsmen

paypal.com.jurisdictionNote: PayPal in the EU operates under PayPal (Europe) S.à r.l., regulated by the Luxembourg CSSF. EU Payment Services Directive provides significantly stronger consumer protections: 2-month advance notice, capped fund holds, no binding arbitration.

credits.title: No binding arbitration

credits.title: Advance notice of changes

spotify.com.jurisdictionNote: Spotify is a Swedish company — GDPR is its primary privacy framework. EU terms are significantly better: no binding arbitration, advance notice of changes, and full GDPR data rights.

flags.title: Location & behavioral tracking

credits.title: Data portability
credits.note: Uber allows data download via Privacy Center

uber.com.jurisdictionNote: Uber's EU entity is Uber B.V. (Amsterdam). No binding arbitration for EU consumers. Location tracking remains a core concern — Uber cannot function without it, but the scope of background tracking is still broad.

airbnb.com.jurisdictionNote: Airbnb Ireland UC is the EU entity. No binding arbitration under EU consumer law. User indemnification is significantly narrowed — EU courts generally reject broad indemnification in consumer contracts.

microsoft.com.jurisdictionNote: Microsoft explicitly states its arbitration clause is 'US only'. EU users face none of it. Microsoft Ireland is the EU entity — GDPR applies with full force.

booking.com.jurisdictionNote: Booking.com B.V. is a Dutch company — GDPR is its home law, not just a compliance checkbox. No binding arbitration for EU consumers. Cookie consent management is available. One of the better EU profiles among marketplaces.
